title: Takeda — Relationships
html:
Takeda -- ZoomRx Relationship

Governing Thought

ZoomRx's relationship with Takeda is a deep, multi-year, steadily diversifying engagement that has evolved from single-brand GI tracking (Gattex PET ~2019-2020) into the most methodologically diverse account in the ZoomRx portfolio — spanning 11 active projects across 5+ TAs with 8+ distinct research methodologies. Unlike J&J (deep within oncology) or Merck (deep within Keytruda), the Takeda relationship is defined by breadth and versatility: Takeda repeatedly returns to ZoomRx for research formats that other vendors either do not offer or cannot execute at the required quality (Color Preference RTs, Radiographic RTs, Conference Studies, Medical Data Assessments, AI Workshops). This positions ZoomRx as a trusted innovation partner, not just a data collection vendor. The strategic question for FY26-27 is whether this breadth can be converted into depth — specifically, whether the zasocitinib and Entyvio franchise opportunities can anchor recurring, multi-year tracking engagements that transform Takeda from a project-based account into a tracker-based account.

Partnership Timeline

Year Milestone
~2019-2020 Relationship established. Gattex PET (Short Bowel Syndrome tracking) and Motegrity PET (chronic constipation). Early GI-focused engagement. Vyvanse RT ParentPulse also ran in 2020 (neuroscience).
2020-2021 Multi-brand expansion. Ninlaro PET (myeloma, oncology), Epilepsy PET (baseline + TBD), Pevonedistat PET (oncology). Partnership extends beyond GI into oncology and neuroscience. Motegrity HCP-Pt Conversations adds a new methodology.
2021-2023 Steady GI tracking. Gattex PET continues across multiple years/waves. Gattex ATU/PCA studies add methodology depth. Millennium/Takeda Onc and GenMed projects suggest cross-BU engagement.
2024 Gattex PET renewal. Gattex PET 2024 and 2024-25 waves run. Relationship deepens in Rare GI.
2025 (H1) Neuroscience Conference Studies emerge. Narcolepsy World Sleep Congress 2025 and SLEEP 2025 conference studies (May-Aug 2025). New methodology category for the account.
2025 (H2) Multi-TA expansion. Rare GI PET 2025-26 (Eohilia tracking). Takeda Psoriasis Radiographic RT (Sep 2025). Plaque Psoriasis Medical Data Feedback Study (Oct 2025). HAE Medical Data Assessment (Oct 2025). Takeda AI Workshop (Oct 2025). Zasocitinib Global Pulse (Nov 2025).
2026 (Q1) Pipeline pre-launch phase. Zasocitinib Color Preference RT (Feb 2026). AAN 2026 conference study (Mar 2026, neuroscience). Mezagitamab Color Preference RT (Apr 2026). Entyvio VERDICT Prelaunch (Apr 2026).

Key pattern: The timeline shows accelerating engagement. From ~2 projects/year in 2019-2021 to 6+ projects in the Oct 2025 - Apr 2026 window alone. The account is in a growth phase driven by Takeda's pipeline maturation (zasocitinib, mezagitamab) and franchise defense (Entyvio, HAE).

Why Takeda Works with ZoomRx

Pillar 1: Methodological Versatility

Takeda's research needs are unusually diverse. Within a single 6-month window (Oct 2025 - Apr 2026), ZoomRx delivered:

  • A PET (Rare GI/Eohilia tracking)
  • A Radiographic RT (Psoriasis)
  • A Medical Data Assessment (HAE)
  • An AI Workshop (cross-TA)
  • A Global Pulse (zasocitinib)
  • Two Color Preference RTs (zasocitinib, mezagitamab)
  • A Conference Study (AAN 2026)
  • A Prelaunch study (Entyvio VERDICT)

No other vendor in ZoomRx's competitive set delivers this range of methodologies to a single client. This versatility is the primary reason Takeda keeps returning — they have one partner who can handle any research format, rather than managing 4-5 specialist vendors.

Pillar 2: Conference Study Capability

ZoomRx has established itself as Takeda's conference research partner, running pre/post surveys across 3+ medical conferences (World Sleep Congress, SLEEP, AAN). Conference studies require rapid turnaround (data collected and analyzed within the conference window), specialized physician targeting (conference attendees), and pre/post methodology design. This is a niche capability that few MR firms execute well. Once established, conference study relationships tend to repeat annually — creating a natural recurring revenue stream.

Pillar 3: Pre-Launch Pipeline Engagement

The zasocitinib and mezagitamab Color Preference RTs, the zasocitinib Global Pulse, and the Entyvio VERDICT prelaunch study all represent pre-launch research. Takeda is engaging ZoomRx before these assets reach market, which positions ZoomRx as the natural tracking partner at launch. This early engagement is the highest-leverage BD activity possible — it creates switching costs before any competitor enters the conversation.

Pillar 4: Novel Methodology Adoption

Takeda is one of very few ZoomRx clients using Color Preference RTs and Radiographic RTs. These niche methodologies — which test visual branding elements and radiographic image interpretation — suggest that Takeda's insights team values creative research design, not just standard survey instruments. This aligns perfectly with ZoomRx's innovation identity.

Proven Impact: Case Studies

Case 1: Gattex PET Multi-Year Tracking (2020-2025)

The Gattex PET engagement ran across 5+ years, tracking promotional effectiveness for Takeda's short bowel syndrome franchise. This is the longest-running ZoomRx engagement on the Takeda account and demonstrates that when PET is established on a brand, it renews. The Gattex relationship also expanded into ATU/PCA, proving the HCPP-to-PET-to-ATU upsell playbook works at Takeda.

BD lesson: PET creates sticky, multi-year revenue. Getting PET established on Entyvio, Takhzyro, or zasocitinib would replicate the Gattex pattern at significantly higher revenue.

Case 2: Narcolepsy Conference Study Series (2025-2026)

Three narcolepsy-related conference studies (World Sleep Congress, SLEEP, AAN 2026) in under 12 months demonstrate Takeda's appetite for rapid-cycle conference research. These studies provide real-time competitive intelligence from medical meetings — a format that traditional tracking studies cannot deliver.

BD lesson: Conference studies are a repeatable, annual revenue stream. Expand to GI (DDW, ACG), derm (AAD), and rare disease conferences to multiply the model.

Case 3: Zasocitinib Multi-Method Pre-Launch Suite (2025-2026)

ZoomRx has delivered a Global Pulse (market landscape), Color Preference RT (branding), and Radiographic RT (psoriasis visual assessment) for zasocitinib — all before the drug reaches market. No competitor has this breadth of pre-launch engagement. When zasocitinib launches, ZoomRx will have 12+ months of pre-launch data context that any new vendor would need years to replicate.

BD lesson: Pre-launch breadth creates post-launch lock-in. The account team should actively pitch PET, ATU, and Message Testing for zasocitinib now, before launch, to secure the tracking mandate.

Case 4: HAE Medical Data Assessment (2025)

The HAE Medical Data Assessment (Oct 2025) delivered a switching topline and AE training materials. This demonstrates ZoomRx's ability to support medical affairs — not just commercial insights — at Takeda. Medical affairs engagement opens a separate budget line and a distinct set of contacts.

BD lesson: Medical affairs is an under-penetrated buyer segment at Takeda. The HAE study is the proof point for expanding into MSL/MEE and medical data work across other TAs.

Growth Trajectory

Near-Term (FY26): Convert Pipeline Engagement to Launch Tracking

The account is at an inflection point. Multiple pre-launch engagements (zasocitinib, mezagitamab, Entyvio VERDICT) will convert to launch tracking engagements as these assets approach market. The account team should be preparing tracking proposals for:

Asset Expected Launch Window Tracking Opportunity
Zasocitinib (PsO) 2027-2028 [UNVERIFIED] PET, ATU, Message Testing, Launch KPI, HCP-Pt, MSL
Zasocitinib (PsA) 2028+ [UNVERIFIED] Same suite, additional indication
Zasocitinib (IBD) 2028-2029 [UNVERIFIED] Same suite; note overlap with Entyvio TA
Mezagitamab (IgAN) 2027-2028 [UNVERIFIED] PET, ATU, Launch KPI, MSL
Entyvio (biosimilar defense) 2025-2026 Competitive PET, switching study, patient retention tracking

Medium-Term (FY27-28): Anchor Recurring Revenue

Convert one-off project relationships into recurring tracker relationships. Priority:

  1. Zasocitinib PET — annual tracking across indications
  2. Entyvio Competitive PET — annual biosimilar monitoring
  3. Conference Study retainer — 4-6 conferences/year across all TAs
  4. MSL/MEE program — recurring tracking across GI, rare disease, neuroscience

Long-Term: Strategic Partnership

Position ZoomRx as Takeda's preferred insights partner across the portfolio. The AI Workshop (Oct 2025) signals interest at the strategic level. If ZoomRx can demonstrate AI-enhanced capabilities (Dynamic Dialogues, AI-powered analysis, Sagan) on one Takeda brand, the potential for an above-brand partnership is real.

Competitive Threats to the Relationship

Threat 1: Vendor Consolidation

Takeda may consolidate MR vendors as part of corporate cost discipline. ZoomRx's engagement is broad but shallow — 11 projects across 5+ TAs, but no single TA has more than 2-3 project types. A vendor consolidation exercise that favors depth over breadth could disadvantage ZoomRx.

Mitigation: Deepen the engagement on at least 2 TAs (GI and Derm/Immunology) to 3+ project types each. This makes ZoomRx harder to replace.

Threat 2: Global/Japan HQ Competition

Some of Takeda's larger research decisions — particularly for global brands like Entyvio and zasocitinib — may involve Japan-HQ sign-off. Global MR firms (Ipsos, Kantar) may have relationships with Takeda Japan that provide an inside track on global mandates.

Mitigation: Lead with ZoomRx's US-specific primary research capability. For global studies (like the zasocitinib Global Pulse), position ZoomRx as the US partner that integrates into global programs rather than competing for the global mandate.

Threat 3: Derm/Immunology Incumbent Vendors

When zasocitinib launches in psoriasis and PsA, Takeda's dermatology and immunology teams may already have incumbent MR vendors (from other brands or from competitive intelligence work). ZoomRx's Color Preference RT and Global Pulse are pre-launch niche work — the commercial tracking mandate may go to a different vendor.

Mitigation: Lock in the zasocitinib PET and ATU proposals before launch. The pre-launch engagement history is ZoomRx's strongest argument for tracking continuity.

Threat 4: Conference Study Competition

Conference study capability is a competitive differentiator today, but other firms are developing similar capabilities. If a competitor offers lower-cost conference research, Takeda could switch.

Mitigation: Build conference study expertise as an integrated service — pre/post surveys plus social listening plus KOL analysis — not just standalone surveys.

AI & Innovation Signals

Signal Detail Source
AI Workshop Takeda ran an AI Workshop with ZoomRx (Oct 2025) with transcripts and an offsite presentation SharePoint scan
Implication Takeda is actively exploring how AI can enhance market research. ZoomRx's Dynamic Dialogues, Sagan, and AI-powered analysis capabilities are directly relevant.
Action Follow up on the AI Workshop with a specific capabilities pitch: "Here is what we showed you in October. Here is how we would apply it to zasocitinib launch research."

Key Takeaway for New Principals

The Takeda account is the inverse of what most ZoomRx accounts look like. Instead of a few services on a few brands (the typical PET-heavy account), Takeda uses many services across many brands. This creates an unusually diversified revenue base and a deep proof-of-concept for ZoomRx's versatility — but it also means there is no single anchor engagement that provides recurring, predictable revenue. The strategic imperative for FY26-27 is to convert this breadth into depth: get PET established on at least one flagship brand (Entyvio, zasocitinib, or Takhzyro), build a conference study retainer, and use the pre-launch zasocitinib engagement as the bridge to a full launch tracking suite. If that conversion happens, Takeda becomes a top-5 account. If it does not, the account remains a collection of one-off projects that requires constant re-selling.

Back to Accounts Hub | Home
